title: Ixora valetonii
taxonomy: Ixora valetonii is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a member of the genus Ixora, which is a group of evergreen shrubs native to tropical and subtropical regions. Ixora valetonii is native to the island of Borneo, specifically in the Malaysian states of Sabah and Sarawak. The plant is known for its clusters of small, fragrant flowers in shades of pink, red, and white. Ixora valetonii is often cultivated for its ornamental value, particularly in tropical gardens.
